_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
clinical_indications: Conduction abnormalities (Atrioventricular block, Bundle branch block/bifascicular block/trifascicular block/intraventricular conduction disease)
Conduction disorders after transcatheter aortic valve replacement

ppd_preauth.q: Clinical notes - detailed history, signs & symptoms, indication for procedure submitted?
Was the ECG and its report by cardiologist submitted?
Was the echo/ colour doppler report submitted?

cpd_claim.q: Are the detailed Procedure / Operative notes submitted?
Is the EP report of the procedure submitted?
Is there a Detailed Discharge Summary mentioning date of follow-up submitted?
Is the invoice/ barcode of catheter submitted?

common_queries: Clinical notes are missing or lack detailed history, signs, symptoms, or indication for the procedure.
ECG with cardiologist report is not submitted or incomplete.
Echo/colour doppler report with stills is not submitted or incomplete.
EP study report is not submitted.
Invoice or barcode of catheters is not submitted at the claim stage.

clinical_key_pointers: Electro-physiological study (EPS) is an invasive procedure that needs catheter placement into the cardiac chambers, commonly via the femoral vein and femoral artery, using the Seldinger technique.
The aim is to stimulate the heart using two pacing techniques: extra-stimulus pacing and incremental pacing.
The use of extra stimulus pacing reveals the refractory periods, conduction and activation changes, diagnostic for certain diseases.

The incremental pacing helps for observing and measuring the impulse conduction during stress conditions and evaluates the recovery time of cardiac tissue under evaluation at cessation of stimulation.

Electrophysiologic testing is indicated in patients with various suspected or obvious arrhythmias for their documentation and initiation, study mechanism (automaticity, triggered activity or re-entry) and treat them by radio frequency ablation (RFA).
